title: Binary Search Implementation
description: Implement the binary search algorithm to efficiently find elements in a sorted array. Binary search is a fundamental divide-and-conquer algorithm with O(log n) time complexity.
instructions: Create a function named `binarySearch` that accepts a sorted array and a target value
Return the index of the target if found, or -1 if not found
Use the divide-and-conquer approach (split the array in half repeatedly)
Compare the middle element with the target to decide which half to search
Continue until the target is found or the search space is empty
learningObjectives: Learn to handle edge cases in searching

hints.content: Initialize two pointers: left = 0 and right = array.length - 1. Use a while loop that continues as long as left <= right.
Calculate the middle index: mid = Math.floor((left + right) / 2). Compare array[mid] with target. If equal, return mid. If target is larger, search the right half by setting left = mid + 1.

If target is smaller than array[mid], search the left half by setting right = mid - 1. If the loop ends without finding the target, return -1. This divide-and-conquer approach eliminates half the search space in each iteration.
